channel.title: AI Model Tracker
channel.description: New additions to the Artificial Analysis LLM Leaderboard

item.title: New model: GPT-5.5 (xhigh) from OpenAI, intel index 60.24
item.description: GPT-5.5 (xhigh) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other models of similar price. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 922k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: GPT-5.5 (high) from OpenAI, intel index 58.87
item.description: GPT-5.5 (high) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other models of similar price. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 922k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: GPT-5.5 (medium) from OpenAI, intel index 56.71
item.description: GPT-5.5 (medium) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other models of similar price. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 922k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: GPT-5.5 (low) from OpenAI, intel index 50.78
item.description: GPT-5.5 (low) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other models of similar price. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 922k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Claude Opus 4.7 (Non-reasoning, High Effort) from Anthropic, intel index 51.82
item.description: Claude Opus 4.7 (Non-reasoning, High Effort) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other non-reasoning models of similar price. It's also notably slow and somewhat verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 1m tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Qwen3.6 Max Preview from Alibaba, intel index 51.81
item.description: Qwen3.6 Max Preview is amongst the leading models in intelligence and well priced when comparing to other models of similar price. The model supports text input, outputs text, and has a 256k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Muse Spark from Meta, intel index 52.15
item.description: Muse Spark is amongst the leading models in intelligence and well priced when comparing to other models of similar price. The model supports text, image, and speech input, outputs text, and has a 262k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: GLM-5.1 (Reasoning) from Z AI, intel index 51.28
item.description: GLM-5.1 (Reasoning) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other open weight models of similar size. It's also faster than average, however very verbose. The model supports text input, outputs text, and has a 200k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Qwen3.5 Omni Plus from Alibaba, intel index 38.63
item.description: Qwen3.5 Omni Plus is amongst the leading models in intelligence and well priced when comparing to other non-reasoning models of similar price. It's also slower than average and very verbose. The model supports text, image, speech, and video input, outputs text and speech, and has a 256k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Gemma 4 26B A4B (Reasoning) from Google, intel index None
item.description: Gemma 4 26B A4B (Reasoning) supports text, image, and video input, outputs text, and has a 256k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Claude Opus 4.6 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort) from Anthropic, intel index 52.95
item.description: Claude Opus 4.6 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other models of similar price. It's also notably slow and very verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 1m tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Qwen3.5 397B A17B (Reasoning) from Alibaba, intel index 45.05
item.description: Qwen3.5 397B A17B (Reasoning) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other open weight models of similar size. It's also notably fast, however very verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 262k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Claude 4 Sonnet (Non-reasoning) from Anthropic, intel index 33
item.description: Claude 4 Sonnet (Non-reasoning) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but somewhat expensive when comparing to other non-reasoning models of similar price. It's also slower than average and somewhat verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 1m tokens context window with knowledge up to March 2025.

item.title: New model: Grok 4 Fast (Reasoning) from xAI, intel index 35.06
item.description: Grok 4 Fast (Reasoning) is amongst the leading models in intelligence and reasonably priced when comparing to other models of similar price. It's also notably fast, however somewhat verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 2m tokens context window.

item.title: New model: MiniMax-M2.7 from MiniMax, intel index 49.62
item.description: MiniMax-M2.7 is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but somewhat expensive when comparing to other models of similar price. It's also notably slow and very verbose. The model supports text input, outputs text, and has a 205k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Claude 4.5 Sonnet (Reasoning) from Anthropic, intel index 43.03
item.description: Claude 4.5 Sonnet (Reasoning) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but somewhat expensive when comparing to other models of similar price. It's also notably slow and very verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 1m tokens context window with knowledge up to July 2025.

item.title: New model: EXAONE 4.0 32B (Non-reasoning) from LG AI Research, intel index 11.66
item.description: EXAONE 4.0 32B (Non-reasoning) is above average in intelligence and well priced when comparing to other open weight non-reasoning models of similar size. The model supports text input, outputs text, and has a 131k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Claude Opus 4.5 (Non-reasoning) from Anthropic, intel index 43.09
item.description: Claude Opus 4.5 (Non-reasoning) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other non-reasoning models of similar price. It's also slower than average and somewhat verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 200k tokens context window with knowledge up to August 2025.

item.title: New model: GPT-5.2 (medium) from OpenAI, intel index 46.64
item.description: GPT-5.2 (medium) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but somewhat expensive when comparing to other models of similar price. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 400k tokens context window with knowledge up to August 2025.

item.title: New model: Kimi K2 Thinking from Kimi, intel index 40.89
item.description: Kimi K2 Thinking is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but somewhat expensive when comparing to other open weight models of similar size. It's also notably fast, however very verbose. The model supports text input, outputs text, and has a 256k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Gemma 3 4B Instruct from Google, intel index 6.3
item.description: Gemma 3 4B Instruct is below average in intelligence, but well priced when comparing to other open weight non-reasoning models of similar size. It's also notably slow, however fairly concise. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 128k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Gemini 3 Deep Think from Google, intel index None
item.description: Gemini 3 Deep Think supports text input, outputs text, and has a 128k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: GLM-5 (Reasoning) from Z AI, intel index 49.77
item.description: GLM-5 (Reasoning) is amongst the leading models in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other open weight models of similar size. It's also faster than average, however very verbose. The model supports text input, outputs text, and has a 200k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Qwen3 30B A3B (Reasoning) from Alibaba, intel index 15.28
item.description: Qwen3 30B A3B (Reasoning) is above average in intelligence, but particularly expensive when comparing to other open weight models of similar size. It's also notably slow and somewhat verbose. The model supports text input, outputs text, and has a 33k tokens context window.

item.title: New model: Llama 4 Scout from Meta, intel index 13.52
item.description: Llama 4 Scout is above average in intelligence and reasonably priced when comparing to other open weight non-reasoning models of similar size. It's also notably fast, however very verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 10m tokens context window with knowledge up to August 2024.

item.title: New model: Llama 4 Maverick from Meta, intel index 18.36
item.description: Llama 4 Maverick is below average in intelligence, but reasonably priced when comparing to other open weight non-reasoning models of similar size. It's also notably fast, however somewhat verbose. The model supports text and image input, outputs text, and has a 1m tokens context window with knowledge up to August 2024.

item.title: New model: Llama 3.2 Instruct 11B (Vision) from Meta, intel index 8.73
item.description: Llama 3.2 Instruct 11B (Vision) is among the least intelligent models and somewhat expensive when comparing to other open weight non-reasoning models of similar size. It's also notably slow and somewhat verbose. The model supports image input, and has a 128k tokens context window with knowledge up to December 2023.

item.title: New model: Llama 3.2 Instruct 90B (Vision) from Meta, intel index 11.90129896307905
item.description: Llama 3.2 Instruct 90B (Vision) is below average in intelligence and somewhat expensive when comparing to other open weight non-reasoning models of similar size. It's also slower than average, however fairly concise. The model supports image input, and has a 128k tokens context window with knowledge up to December 2023.
